reason: Answer A mischaracterizes default-alive status as fragile ("one bad quarter kills you"), which is backwards - default-alive means the company survives without more funding if trends hold, a position of strength/leverage. Answer B correctly uses default-alive status to establish negotiating leverage, and additionally covers dilution math, use-of-funds framing, founder-fit (venture path vs lifestyle business), and a concrete decision test. B is more accurate and more useful without being padded.
